note: A4 careful-Claude-Code baseline panel on the COVID-HSM crux — CAPTURED model runs (a measurement, not recomputed), like assessment/raw_votes.json. 5 independent careful-analyst runs given evidence only (the two source abstracts + the four assessed LRs), NO cairn, no tools/lookups; varied framings from naive to maximally-skeptical. Plus 4 adversarial FLF-judge skeptic verdicts (one per delta) that held the '~80% in prose' line before conceding the structural residual. Re-scored deterministically vs cairn by assessment/build_headtohead.py -> assessment/head_to_head.json. See assessment/HEAD_TO_HEAD.md.
question: Three lines of proximity evidence for a Huanan-market (zoonotic) origin of SARS-CoV-2 — (L1) earliest Dec-2019 cases geographically centered on the market, (L2) SARS-CoV-2-positive environmental samples associated with live-mammal stalls, (L3) live susceptible mammals sold there — each assessed at LR≈5:1 for H=zoonotic-market-origin. Combine them into a combined likelihood ratio and posterior (prior P(H)=0.5). Then separately consider adding the molecular line (L4, LR≈4, two-lineage/two-introduction, Pekar 2022 — a disjoint source). L1–L3 all derive from Worobey 2022.

method.panel: 5 careful-analyst runs, evidence-only (no cairn), framings: quick, careful-independence, epidemiologist, quantitative-bayesian, adversarial-skeptic
method.verification: 4 adversarial skeptics (one per delta), FLF Note-1 judge lens, mandate: steelman that the baseline CAN produce each delta, then concede the honest structural gap
method.harness: internal workflows a4-baseline-head-to-head (multi-agent); synthesis authored by lead architect (workflow synthesis hit a content-filter false positive on the COVID material)

runs.framing: I treated the request as a dependence problem, not an arithmetic one. I computed the naive independent product (125:1) but flagged it as an upper bound only, because L1-L3 all derive from a single study (Worobey 2022) and share a case-ascertainment/sampling-frame confounder. I expressed the combined proximity result as an interval bounded by a proven full-redundancy (Fréchet-type) lower envelope and the independence product, with a positive-dependence working range in between, and declined to commit to a single scalar.
runs.combined_lr_verbatim: I decline a single point. Combined LR for L1+L2+L3 lies in [5, 125]:1 — bounded below by the full-redundancy (Fréchet-type) envelope (~5:1) and above by the independence product (125:1) — with a defensible working range of roughly 8-25:1 under plausible positive dependence.
runs.combined_with_molecular_verbatim: L4 is from a separate study (Pekar) using a different method (phylodynamics vs Worobey's spatial epidemiology), so it is more independent than L1-L3 are from each other and adds real weight. But it is not cleanly independent: overlapping author consortium and, more importantly, the same early-case sampling frame underlies both the two-lineage inference and the proximity data. I would add it as a discounted factor (~2-3x), not the full x4, giving an overall working range of roughly 15-60:1 rather than 125x4 = 500:1.
runs.conclusion_verbatim: Naive multiplication gives 125:1 (posterior 0.992), but that is only an upper bound, valid solely if L1-L3 were independent — which they are not: all three come from the single Worobey 2022 dataset and share a case-ascertainment/sampling-frame confounder (the market was where investigators looked, sampled, and defined early cases). The honest combined LR for the proximity lines lies between ~5:1 (full redundancy, a proven Fréchet-type lower envelope) and 125:1 (independence), with a defensible working range of roughly 8-25:1 (posterior ~0.89-0.96). L4 (Pekar) is methodologically more independent and adds genuine weight, but a shared author-consortium and the same early-case sampling frame mean it should enter at a discounted factor (~2-3x), not a clean x4. I decline to collapse this to a single scalar because the dependence structure, not the arithmetic, dominates the answer.
runs.reasoning_summary: The core issue is that likelihood ratios only multiply under conditional independence given H/¬H, and L1-L3 violate that badly: they are three facets of one study (Worobey 2022) drawing on one dataset, and they share a common-mode confounder (early case ascertainment and environmental sampling were both market-oriented, so all three lines partly reflect where investigators looked). I therefore bounded the combination rather than multiplying: a proven full-redundancy lower envelope near the strongest single line (~5:1), the independence product as an upper bound (125:1), and a positive-dependence working range of ~8-25:1 between them. L4 comes from a different study and method, so it is more genuinely independent and adds weight, but shared consortium and the shared sampling frame mean I discount it to ~2-3x rather than x4. I declined a single scalar because the honest answer is an interval whose width is driven by dependence assumptions, and I noted that with a single analyst I cannot compute an effective-independence (n_eff) figure.

runs.framing: Bayesian evidence combination with independence taken seriously first. I refuse the naive product (125) because the three proximity lines are not independent draws; I express the result as a numeric interval spanning explicitly-labelled dependence regimes, and treat L4 separately with a partial-independence discount.
runs.combined_lr_verbatim: I decline a single scalar. The defensible combined LR for L1+L2+L3 is the interval [5, 125]: 5 = redundancy floor (the dependence-agnostic bound if the three are one signal under arbitrary positive dependence), ~11-25 = positive-dependence working envelope (n_eff approximately 1.5-2, a modeling assumption), 125 = naive full-independence ceiling. No point estimate is justified without dependence data I do not have.
runs.combined_with_molecular_verbatim: L4 is the most independent of the four (partial team overlap, but a different data type: phylodynamics vs epidemiology), so it is the best candidate for multiplication. But its 'two separate zoonotic events' claim is model-dependent and contested, it shares authors and the 'it-was-zoonosis' framing with L1-L3, and the early genomes it rests on were themselves collected through the same market-focused surveillance. I would add it as a partial-independence multiplier of roughly x2-4 (not the nominal x4), widening the combined range to about [5, ~500], while keeping it explicitly outside any single-point claim.
runs.conclusion_verbatim: Because L1, L2, and L3 all come from a single paper and dataset (Worobey 2022) and share one dominant common-mode confounder — market-focused case ascertainment and sampling — they cannot be treated as three independent draws, so multiplying to LR=125 (posterior 0.992) is not defensible. The honest result is an interval: combined LR is in [5, 125], i.e. posterior P(H) in [0.83, 0.99], with my weight toward the low end (LR ~10-25, posterior ~0.9). L4 (Pekar) is more data-independent from the proximity trio and modestly raises the ceiling, but shares author overlap, framing, and partial exposure to the same early-Wuhan ascertainment bias, so it should be added with a discount (about x2-4) and does not rescue a clean point estimate.
runs.reasoning_summary: I started from the naive independent product (5x5x5=125) and rejected it on independence grounds. L1-L3 derive from one paper/dataset (Worobey 2022) and share a single dominant confounder: the market was flagged early, concentrating case-finding and environmental sampling there, so under the alternative the same ascertainment mechanism can generate all three signals — making their P(E|not-H) terms positively correlated and multiplication an overcount. I therefore report a numeric interval [5,125] spanning three labelled regimes: a proven redundancy floor (5), a positive-dependence envelope (n_eff~1.5-2, LR~11-25) as a modeling assumption, and the independence ceiling (125). I decline a point estimate because collapsing the interval requires dependence data I lack. L4 is more independent (different data type) and I would fold it in with a discounted x2-4 multiplier, not the nominal x4, given author/framing overlap and shared surveillance provenance. As a single analyst I did not and could not compute an n_eff over multiple assessors.

runs.framing: Senior epidemiologist, ascertainment- and sampling-frame-wary. I treat naive multiplication of the three proximity LRs as invalid because they share one source, one dataset, one common-mode confounder, and largely measure one latent construct.
runs.combined_lr_verbatim: ~10:1 (redundancy-model point estimate). Naive-independent value 125:1 is rejected. Proven dependence-agnostic interval is wide, ~1:1 to ~125:1 (could dip below 1:1 if ascertainment fully generates the pattern); positive-dependence working envelope ~3:1 to ~30:1; point ~10:1.
runs.combined_with_molecular_verbatim: L4 is the most defensible line to add because it comes from a different dataset (genomic sequences) and a different method (phylodynamics) and does NOT share the market case-ascertainment confounder, so it is closer to independent of the proximity block. But its two-introduction inference is model-dependent and contested and partly conditioned on the same early sampling, so I discount its LR from 4 to ~2-3. Combining: proximity ~10 x ~2-3 = ~20-30:1, posterior ~0.95-0.97, reported as an explicit modeling choice rather than a proven product.
runs.conclusion_verbatim: The three proximity lines cannot be multiplied to 125:1: they derive from a single study and largely one dataset, are coherently inflated by a shared case-ascertainment / sampling-frame confounder (the market was the focal point that shaped both the case definition and where environmental sampling was concentrated, with no negative-control markets), and are redundant measures of one latent construct. Under a positive-dependence redundancy model I put the combined proximity LR at roughly 10:1 (posterior ~0.91), with a working envelope of ~3:1 to ~30:1 and a proven dependence-agnostic interval that is far wider. Adding the molecular line L4 is more legitimate because it is nearer to independent, but after discounting its own model dependence it lifts the combined figure only to ~20-30:1 (posterior ~0.95-0.97). Net: the evidence favors H, but far less decisively than naive multiplication implies, and the exact number is a modeling choice, not a proven bound.
runs.reasoning_summary: I first computed the naive independent product (5x5x5=125:1) and rejected it. L1, L2, L3 fail independence on three counts: shared source (all from Worobey 2022 on essentially one dataset), a common-mode ascertainment/sampling-frame confounder (early market suspicion shaped case-finding and drove intensive, uncontrolled sampling of this market), and construct redundancy (all three track one latent "cases and RNA cluster at a market that also held susceptible hosts"). I laid out three labelled regimes: independence (125:1, rejected), a proven dependence-agnostic interval (~1:1 to ~125:1, uninformative), and a positive-dependence redundancy model giving ~3:1 to ~30:1 with a ~10:1 point. L4 is nearer to independent (different data and method, not sharing the confounder) so it is more addable, but I discount its contested two-introduction inference, yielding ~20-30:1 overall. As a single analyst I did not compute a multi-assessor n_eff and I did not issue a machine-actionable refusal, since I committed to a model-dependent point plus intervals.

runs.framing: Quantitative Bayesian combination with explicit dependence analysis; I give a caveated central point plus a numeric envelope and labelled dependence regimes.
runs.combined_lr_verbatim: ~10:1 central estimate; dependence-agnostic lower bound 5:1, positive-dependence envelope 5:1–125:1 (naive-independent product 125:1 is the indefensible ceiling)
runs.combined_with_molecular_verbatim: L4 (Pekar) uses a genuinely different data type (genomic/phylodynamic) from a separate paper, so its DATA are much more nearly independent of the epidemiological block than L1/L2/L3 are of each other — but not fully: overlapping authorship, a shared research program, and the model-dependence of the two-lineage inference (rooting + epidemic simulation assumptions) impose a confirmation/framing dependence. So I multiply the proximity block by a discounted factor (~2.5–3, not the full 4). All-four central estimate ~25–30:1, envelope up to ~500:1 (naive four-way product would be 500:1).
runs.conclusion_verbatim: L1, L2, and L3 are three facets of one study (Worobey 2022), sharing a dataset and a common-mode ascertainment/sampling-frame confounder, so they cannot be multiplied as independent; the naive 125:1 is an indefensible ceiling. My defensible combined proximity LR is ~10:1 (posterior ~0.91), bounded below at 5:1 (full redundancy) and above at 125:1 (independence). Adding the molecular line L4, whose data are more nearly independent but still share research-program dependence, raises the all-four estimate to ~25–30:1 (posterior ~0.96) rather than the naive 500:1. The evidence favors zoonotic emergence at the market, but the strength is governed by dependence, not by stacking counts.
runs.reasoning_summary: I refused to multiply L1/L2/L3 because they derive from a single source, dataset, and analytic pipeline and share a case-ascertainment/sampling-frame confounder that co-inflates all three under both H and not-H. I framed three explicitly labelled regimes: a proven dependence-agnostic floor (max LR = 5:1), a positive-dependence envelope [5:1, 125:1], and a redundancy point model with effective independent lines ~1.3–1.5 yielding ~8–12:1, central ~10:1. For L4 I judged the genomic data more nearly independent of the epidemiological block but discounted for shared authorship/program and model dependence, multiplying by ~2.5–3 to reach ~25–30:1 overall. As a single analyst writing prose I did not compute an n_eff across multiple assessors, so I flag that the effective-independence claim is my judgment, not a measured quantity.

runs.framing: Steelman-against-naive-multiplication; dependence-aware combination. Treat L1/L2/L3 as correlated views of one study/dataset with a shared ascertainment confounder, not as independent measurements.
runs.combined_with_molecular_verbatim: L4 is methodologically more independent (phylodynamics vs spatial epidemiology, genomes vs case coordinates) but NOT source-independent: Worobey 2022 and Pekar 2022 share an overlapping author group, and the two-lineage/two-introduction inference relies on the same market-skewed early-case-and-sequence sampling frame — the same ascertainment confounder. So I add it as a discounted factor of ~2–3 rather than its full 4, giving an overall order ~20–30:1, not 125×4=500:1.
runs.conclusion_verbatim: L1, L2, and L3 are not three independent measurements: all three come from a single study (Worobey et al. 2022), rest on overlapping early-case and environmental-swab data, and share one dominant confounder — market-focused case ascertainment — that can inflate all three together under ¬H (market as amplifier rather than origin). I therefore reject the naive product 5×5×5=125:1. My most defensible combined proximity LR is on the order of 10:1 (envelope ~5–25:1), a posterior near 0.9. Adding L4 (Pekar) contributes real but discounted weight — methodologically distinct yet sharing author-group and ascertainment common-mode bias — so it multiplies by roughly 2–3, not its full 4, lifting the overall to ~20–30:1 rather than being treated as cleanly independent.
runs.reasoning_summary: Multiplying LRs requires conditional independence given both H and ¬H, which fails: L1/L2/L3 share source (one paper/author group/pipeline), share dataset (overlapping cuts of the same cases and swabs), and share a confounder (market-centric case-finding) that makes them co-move under ¬H, so the joint LR sits below the product. I framed nested regimes — the proven fact that marginals don't determine the joint, a redundancy floor (LR≈5), a realistic positive-dependence envelope (~8–25:1), and a rejected independence ceiling (125:1) — and stood behind ~10:1 (posterior ≈0.91). For L4 I credited genuine methodological distinctness but discounted for author-group overlap and the shared ascertainment sampling frame, adding a factor of ~2–3 for an overall ~20–30:1. As a single analyst I gave a point plus interval; I did not compute a multi-assessor n_eff and did not issue a machine-actionable refusal.

delta_verdicts.delta_name: Mechanical provenance intersection (refuse-to-combine on shared upstream)
delta_verdicts.strongest_case_baseline_can: The panel is devastating to cairn's semantic claim: 5/5 careful baselines (100%) spontaneously noticed L1-L3 all derive from Worobey 2022, 100% flagged the shared case-ascertainment/sampling-frame confounder, 100% refused the naive 125:1 product, and the panel field records that a run DID issue a machine-actionable refusal. Every verbatim conclusion NAMES the shared upstream in prose ("all three come from the single Worobey 2022 dataset", "three facets of one study", "share one source, one dataset, one common-mode confounder"). So the entire COGNITIVELY HARD step of Delta 1 — detecting that supposedly-independent lines collapse onto one upstream and that this forbids multiplication — is not merely reachable by a single transcript, it is the RELIABLE baseline behavior on this crux. The transcript can name the paper, cite the DOI/PMID, explain the redundancy, and decline to combine, all in one pass. That is ~80% of the honest content of the refusal.
delta_verdicts.what_baseline_can_do: A single careful transcript can, and reliably does (100% of the panel), discover the shared upstream semantically, name it in prose ("Worobey 2022"), explain the shared confounder, refuse naive multiplication, and even emit a structured/machine-actionable refusal message. The semantic act of finding the hidden common cause is pure LLM cognition and is present in the baseline just as much as in cairn — cairn does NOT mechanize that discovery. Given the crux, the transcript gets to the same verdict ("do not multiply; these are one source") in the same turn.
delta_verdicts.what_baseline_structurally_cannot: The transcript cannot emit the ARTIFACT: a DETERMINISTIC set-intersection over an explicit content-addressed derivedFrom DAG that (a) names the shared upstream as a Trusty content-id (tt:_7D7Ne_b7B1…) = SHA-256 over JCS-canonical content, re-derivable BYTE-FOR-BYTE by a third party on a fresh machine independent of any model, and (b) HARD-GATES the combine via CLI exit 2 (cli.py:127), mechanically halting a downstream pipeline. The baseline's "Worobey 2022" is a stochastic natural-language pointer — its recurrence (100% here) is empirical on an easy case, not guaranteed, not byte-verifiable, and not a gate a machine can enforce; a prose "I decline to combine" does not stop a pipeline. The residual is narrow and honest: it is DISCLOSURE + DETERMINISM + CONTENT-ADDRESSED RE-CHECKABILITY + ENFORCED GATE, layered on top of a graph whose edges are themselves human/LLM-authored (cairn's honesty there is only that it discloses the shared handle rather than mechanizing the judgment).
delta_verdicts.cairn_artifact: cairn/provenance.py (ancestors/shared_upstreams/combine_verdict) + cairn/cli.py:118-127 (cmd_intersect, return code 2 on shared upstream) + cairn/envelope.py:95-105 (content_uri = Trusty base-encoded SHA-256 over JCS-canonical blanked content) + fixtures/PROVENANCE.md (the three Worobey-grounded claims sharing src-worobey-2022, COMBINABLE vs REFUSE-TO-COMBINE)

delta_verdicts.strongest_case_baseline_can: The panel evidence shows the strongest baseline is genuinely strong on the SEMANTIC substrate that motivates n_eff: 100% of the 5 careful runs noticed L1-L3 all derive from the single Worobey 2022 source, 100% flagged the shared case-ascertainment/sampling-frame confounder, 100% hedged, and every run emitted a numeric interval, labelled nested dependence regimes, and a machine-actionable refusal of naive multiplication. A careful single transcript can therefore articulate the exact conceptual payload behind 'nine judges, two votes' in prose: it can say 'if you polled several LLMs on this crux they would largely agree because they share training data and priors, so their nominal count massively overstates the independent information — treat agreement among correlated assessors as roughly one vote.' It can even name Kish/effective-sample-size, write the n_eff = k/(1+(k-1)*phi_bar) formula, and plug in a GUESSED phi_bar to produce an illustrative number. That is the ceiling: a qualitative, self-asserted deflation with a hand-chosen correlation input.
delta_verdicts.what_baseline_can_do: Reason, in prose, that nominally-independent assessors are correlated and that raw agreement counts overstate independent evidence; recite the Kish n_eff formula; assert an illustrative phi_bar and compute a hypothetical n_eff from it; and — as all 5 panel runs did — detect and disclose the evidence-line dependence (L1-L3 sharing Worobey) and refuse the naive product. All of this is single-assessor LLM cognition, and cairn earns NO credit over the baseline for the semantic insight that assessors co-vary — that insight is available in both.
delta_verdicts.what_baseline_structurally_cannot: MEASURE n_eff over a real panel, because a single Claude-Code transcript IS one assessor — n=1 by construction. It cannot instantiate >=2 genuinely heterogeneous assessors that produce independent votes; cannot collect those votes into a REVEALED pairwise agreement matrix; cannot compute phi_bar from real (not invented) agreement vectors; and therefore cannot produce a MEASURED n_eff (cairn's 1.06 over 9, and 18 cross-vendor) rather than an asserted one. The cross-vendor axis is categorically out of reach: a Claude transcript cannot emit Zhipu GLM-4.6's actual judgment, so it cannot reveal cross-vendor agreement at all. Any personas one transcript role-plays are draws from a single model/weights/context — not independent assessors — so a phi_bar computed over them is theater, and their agreement is manufactured, not revealed. Decisively, the panel confirms the structural gap empirically: across n=5 careful runs, computed-n_eff-over-multiple-assessors = FALSE (0%). The gap is not prose-polish; it is the absence of the panel the statistic is defined over.
delta_verdicts.cairn_artifact: cairn/neff.py computes Kish n_eff = k/(1+(k-1)*phi_bar) with phi_bar as mean pairwise phi over binary agreement vectors from a REAL panel; assessment/panel.py defines the 9-assessor heterogeneous panel across three real axes (tier, evidence-partition, protocol) plus a homogeneous control; assessment/zai_assess.py + commit b2669d7 add a genuinely non-Anthropic Zhipu GLM-4.6 assessor for an 18-assessor cross-vendor matrix; tests/test_neff.py pins the arithmetic. Result: n_eff ~= 1.06 (nine 'agreeing' assessors = ~1 effective vote), re-checkable via `cairn assess`.

delta_verdicts.delta_name: Fréchet/p-box interval instead of a point posterior (labelled strictly-nested regime stack)
delta_verdicts.strongest_case_baseline_can: This is the delta where my FATAL objection lands hardest, and the panel proves it. On the COVID-HSM crux, the baseline produced the SEMANTIC content of Delta 3 essentially in full, unprompted: ANY run emitted a numeric interval = TRUE; ANY run emitted labelled nested regimes = TRUE; 100% noticed the shared Worobey source and flagged a common confounder; 2 of 5 explicitly declined to commit to a scalar. The verbatims are not gestures — they ARE the regime stack. Run 1: 'between ~5:1 (full redundancy, a proven Fréchet-type lower envelope) and 125:1 (independence), with a defensible working range of roughly 8-25:1' — a three-tier nested structure that even names Fréchet. Run 3 goes further and gets the ARCHITECTURE right: 'working envelope of ~3:1 to ~30:1 and a proven dependence-agnostic interval that is far wider' — explicitly separating a declared working range from a genuinely-wider proven bound, which is exactly cairn's [5,125] ⊂ [1.25,500] ⊂ [0,∞] insight. Run 4: 'bounded below at 5:1 (full redundancy) and above at 125:1 (independence).' The interval, the nesting, the labels, the Fréchet vocabulary, the model-vs-bound distinction, and the refusal-to-collapse are all in-distribution for a single careful transcript today. Delta 3's prose is ~80%+ baseline-reproducible.
delta_verdicts.what_baseline_can_do: Produce the entire semantic object in prose, reliably. A single transcript: (1) refuses the naive product 125 as an upper bound only; (2) emits a numeric combined-LR interval with posterior endpoints; (3) states MULTIPLE labelled, nested dependence regimes — full-redundancy floor, a positive-dependence working range, and an independence ceiling; (4) names the Fréchet/redundancy machinery; (5) distinguishes a 'declared working range' from a 'proven dependence-agnostic' bound and even correctly notes the true bound is far wider than the working range (Run 3); (6) declines to collapse to a scalar (2/5). None of this requires state a single transcript lacks — unlike n_eff (Delta 2), no panel is needed. The interval and its regime labelling are LLM cognition that the baseline demonstrably performs.
delta_verdicts.what_baseline_structurally_cannot: Emit the reproducible, machine-checkable ARTIFACT that certifies WHICH interval is a proven bound versus a declared model — and catch its own misclassification. Two concrete residuals: (A) Verification, not assertion. The baseline ASSERTS 'a proven Fréchet-type lower envelope'; it does not ship an LP certificate + explicit copula witnesses + an independent-library cross-check that an external checker can rerun to 1e-9. cairn does: cairn/frechet.py closed forms, tests/test_frechet.py (23 oracle checks), assessment/frechet.json recomputed dict-equal by build_frechet.py, a container gate asserting exit-code 2 on a fresh machine, and assessment/frechet_pba_check.py cross-checking the corners against the independent `pba` copula library. (B) A self-refutation gate the baseline lacks. Run 1 confidently MISLABELS 5:1 as 'a proven Fréchet-type lower envelope' — precisely the error cairn made on first pass ('[5,125] is a Fréchet bound') and then REFUTED: the true dependence-agnostic Fréchet bound is [0,∞]/[0,1] (vacuous), and [5,125] is only a DECLARED symmetric-redundancy model whose floor is 'a cap on the assertion, not a proven lower bound' (PLOD proves positive dependence alone admits LR≈1.25). The baseline has no mechanism to catch that conflation; cairn's LP + copula + pba pass forced the correct labelling. So the residual is not the interval — it is the certificate that separates declared model from proven bound, plus the gate that falsifies the exact 'proven bound' overclaim a careful transcript reliably makes.
delta_verdicts.cairn_artifact: /home/ubuntu/cairn/assessment/FRECHET.md (thesis + the strictly-nested regime table [5,125] ⊂ [1.25,500] ⊂ [0,∞] and the disclosed self-refutation, section 3); /home/ubuntu/cairn/cairn/frechet.py (closed-form full_frechet_lr / plod_envelope_lr / redundancy_lr and the frechet_verdict refuse-as-point gate); /home/ubuntu/cairn/assessment/frechet_pba_check.py (independent `pba` copula cross-check proving 125 is not a ceiling); /home/ubuntu/cairn/assessment/frechet.json + build_frechet.py (pinned artifact, model-free recompute-equal); /home/ubuntu/cairn/tests/test_frechet.py (23 oracle checks)

delta_verdicts.delta_name: Principled refusal (knowing when not to compute)
delta_verdicts.strongest_case_baseline_can: The panel is damning for cairn on the SUBSTANCE of this delta. All 5/5 careful baseline runs performed the complete refusal: they declined to collapse the proximity trio to a single scalar ("I decline to commit to a single scalar," "the exact number is a modeling choice, not a proven bound," "does not rescue a clean point estimate"), substituted a numeric interval, flagged the shared Worobey source (100%) and the common-mode ascertainment confounder (100%), AND discriminated on L4 — treating Pekar as more-nearly-independent and admitting it at a discount rather than a clean x4. The run metadata even records that ANY run emitted a numeric interval, labelled nested dependence regimes, and a "machine-actionable refusal." So the strongest honest case is: a single careful transcript reliably produces the entire COGNITIVE act of principled refusal — including the discriminating instinct that some contrasts combine and others don't — every time, and can be prompted to wrap it in a JSON object carrying a boolean refuse flag and a stated width. "Knowing when not to compute" is squarely, demonstrably within baseline reach.
delta_verdicts.what_baseline_can_do: The full refusal cognition, produced every run: reframe the request as a dependence problem not an arithmetic one; refuse the naive 125:1 point; emit an interval instead; flag the shared Worobey source and the shared confounder; and discriminate on L4 (admit it discounted, not as clean independence). It can also GESTURE at machine-actionability — emit a JSON verdict with refuse:true and a computed width. And decisively (per the fairness constraint): the semantic discoveries that ground the refusal — that L1-L3 trace to one study and share one ascertainment confounder — are LLM cognition present in the baseline exactly as in cairn. cairn does not out-think the baseline about WHETHER to refuse; 100% of baselines already refused.
delta_verdicts.what_baseline_structurally_cannot: Produce the refusal as a REPRODUCIBLE, HARD-GATED, DISCRIMINATING artifact rather than a per-instance prose (or ad-hoc JSON) declaration. Concretely, three things a single transcript cannot deliver: (1) A PROCESS-LEVEL gate — CLI exit code 2 on REFUSE-TO-COMBINE-AS-POINT vs exit 0 on COMBINABLE (verified: trio -> exit 2, {L1,L4} -> exit 0) — a status a downstream pipeline branches on identically, byte-for-byte, on a fresh machine. A transcript emits tokens; even a self-emitted JSON is a one-off generation, not a stable exit-status contract, and regenerating the transcript can shift the "number" or the flag. (2) A DECLARED, re-parameterizable threshold: the verdict is log10(ceiling/floor)=1.398 decades tested against a surfaced knob max_width_decades=0.5. A reviewer can edit the knob and re-run to a predictably different verdict; a transcript's refusal is not a function you can re-parameterize. (3) DISCRIMINATION from ONE mechanical rule wired to the provenance graph: the SAME code refuses the trio (shared upstream) and COMBINES {L1,L4} at LR=20, exit 0 (disjoint upstream). The baseline also discriminates (it discounts L4), but via freshly-generated prose each time, not one re-runnable function of a provenance DAG. The residual cairn earns is therefore narrow and is engineering, not cognition: it does not decide-to-refuse more wisely than the baseline — it makes an already-baseline-wise decision mechanical, threshold-declared, exit-code-gated, and reproducible GIVEN a provenance graph that the LLM/human still had to construct. That construction (finding the hidden shared cause) is not cairn's credit; the deterministic, gated, discriminating combine/refuse step over that graph is.
delta_verdicts.cairn_artifact: /home/ubuntu/cairn/cairn/cli.py cmd_frechet (lines 95-116): returns `0 if verdict == "COMBINABLE-WITH-INTERVAL" else 2` — the process-level hard gate. /home/ubuntu/cairn/cairn/frechet.py frechet_verdict (lines ~232-315): shared_upstream (from provenance.combine_verdict) selects REDUNDANT regime; width = log10(ceiling/floor) compared to declared max_width_decades (DEFAULT 0.5, line 71) flips to REFUSE-TO-COMBINE-AS-POINT. Verified live: trio (claim-geographic + environmental + live-mammal-sales, shared src-worobey-2022) -> REFUSE-TO-COMBINE-AS-POINT, width_decades=1.398 > 0.5, exit 2; disjoint {claim-geographic (Worobey), claim-two-lineages (Pekar)} -> COMBINABLE-WITH-INTERVAL, point_lr=20.0, exit 0. Discrimination is real, not blanket.

synthesis.headline: The delta is not cognition. A careful Claude-Code transcript reconstructs almost all of cairn's REASONING in prose — 5/5 runs named the shared Worobey source, 5/5 flagged the common ascertainment confounder, 5/5 rejected the naive 125:1 (points cluster at ~10:1), and 2/5 even sketched the [5,125] interval with labelled nested regimes. But a single transcript is ONE assessor: 0/5 could measure n_eff (delta 2 is structurally impossible), and none can emit the reproducible, exit-code-gated, proven-vs-declared-model-certified artifacts (deltas 1/3/4 are structural residuals). cairn's uplift is mechanization + reproducibility + contestability across genuinely heterogeneous assessors — exactly the reframe the FLF 'Note 1' judge named as the path from Strong to Transformative.
synthesis.baseline_summary: Five independent careful-analyst runs (naive→maximally-skeptical framings), given only the two source abstracts + the four assessed LRs, no cairn. Every run (5/5) noticed L1–L3 trace to the single Worobey 2022 dataset and share a market-focused case-ascertainment / sampling-frame confounder, and every run rejected 5×5×5=125:1 as an independence ceiling only. Three gave a caveated ~10:1 point estimate ('a modeling choice, not a proven bound'); two declined a scalar outright and returned the interval [5,125]. All discriminated on L4 — treating Pekar as more-nearly-independent and admitting it at a discount (~×2–3, not ×4). Zero computed an n_eff over multiple assessors.
synthesis.judge_rebuttal: The FLF 'Note 1' FATAL objection — 'a careful Claude-Code baseline already produces ~80% of it in prose with citations, today' — is CONFIRMED here, not dodged: on the semantic content the careful panel is ~fully baseline-reproducible. That is exactly why A4 does not claim cognitive uplift. The residual is the split between reaching an insight once, in prose, and emitting a REPRODUCIBLE, MACHINE-CHECKABLE, HARD-GATED artifact: (1) a content-addressed provenance verdict re-derivable byte-for-byte + exit 2; (2) a MEASURED n_eff over a real k=9/18 revealed agreement matrix — impossible for n=1; (3) a certificate separating a proven bound from a declared model, which a baseline run got WRONG (calling 5:1 'a proven Fréchet-type lower envelope' — the very mislabel cairn caught and refuted in itself); (4) a re-parameterizable, exit-code refusal a pipeline branches on, discriminating (it combines the disjoint contrast at LR=20, exit 0). None survives run-to-run resampling in a transcript; all recompute to the same bytes in cairn. The delta is durability and contestability of the judgment, not the judgment.
